meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate)
content.seo_description: Learn how to use Git with AI to build a portfolio. This intermediate guide uses Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your development and career growth.
content.answer_block: Transform your GitHub into a high-value asset by using AI-driven development workflows. Integrate Claude Code for rapid feature iteration and Antigravity to architect scalable systems. By documenting your AI-assisted build process, you prove to recruiters that you can leverage modern engineering tools to ship production-ready, professional-grade software.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the student's greatest adversary. You have the theoretical knowledge, but your GitHub remains a ghost town, failing to capture the complexity of the systems you are actually capable of building. The industry doesn't care about your GPA; it cares about your ability to solve problems and ship code at speed.

We are entering an era where engineering is redefined by AI-human collaboration. By mastering the synergy between Git, Antigravity, and Claude Code, you stop being a student and start being a builder. This guide bridges the gap between 'knowing how to code' and 'demonstrating professional engineering competence' through a structured, AI-augmented workflow.

steps.title: Architecting the Repository Structure
steps.description: Establish a professional foundation by generating a modular project architecture that demonstrates scalability and clean code principles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a professional project structure for a full-stack SaaS application using TypeScript. Include folders for services, models, controllers, and tests. Ensure the architecture follows Domain Driven Design patterns.

steps.title: Automated Feature Branching
steps.description: Use AI to define atomic tasks that translate into meaningful, incremental Git commits, showing a history of intentional progress.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project requirements and break them down into 5 discrete, atomic features. Suggest a Git branching strategy for each task.
steps.claude_code_command: claude task 'implement user authentication module' --branch feat/auth-service

steps.title: AI-Assisted Code Documentation
steps.description: Ensure your commit history is readable by generating professional commit messages and inline documentation that explains the 'why' behind the code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a professional git commit message following Conventional Commits specification for the changes made in the auth-service branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude git commit --message 'feat(auth): implement JWT-based session management'

steps.title: Automated Testing and Validation
steps.description: Use Claude to write unit tests for your features before you merge, ensuring your portfolio showcases high-quality, bug-resistant code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Jest test suite for the newly created user registration controller, covering success cases and edge case validation.

steps.title: Production-Ready README Generation
steps.description: Your README is your landing page. Use AI to synthesize your technical decisions into a compelling narrative for recruiters.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a README.md file that highlights the engineering challenges solved, the tech stack, and the AI-assisted development workflow used in this project.

pro_tips: Always include a 'Lessons Learned' section in your README; recruiters value engineers who reflect on their technical trade-offs.
Use AI to identify performance bottlenecks in your code before pushing; it shows you prioritize optimization.
Tag your repository with specific industry keywords like 'SaaS', 'Scalable', and 'FullStack' to increase visibility in search.
Keep your pull requests small and focused; large, monolithic PRs are a red flag for senior engineering teams.
Link your project to a live demo environment; a hosted app is worth ten times more than a repo of static code.
content.next_steps: Deploy your application to a platform like Vercel or Railway, update your portfolio site with the live link, and share your project on LinkedIn with a breakdown of the specific AI tools you used to build it.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' project and start shipping the one that gets you hired. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
